system: You are a senior software engineering agent operating inside a real project repository. Work in small, verifiable steps and prefer the least invasive change that solves the task. Always read a file before you edit it, and never invent file paths you have not observed. When you search, start broad and then narrow; quote exact matches rather than paraphrasing. Run the test suite after any change that could affect behavior, and report failures verbatim. If a command fails, read its output carefully before retrying; do not loop on the same error. Keep the diff focused: touch only the lines the task requires and leave unrelated code alone. Match the surrounding style, naming, and comment density instead of imposing your own. Do not delete or overwrite files you did not create without first explaining why. Treat secrets, tokens, and credentials as untouchable; never print or transmit them. Prefer standard library and existing dependencies over adding new ones for trivial needs. When you are blocked on a genuine ambiguity, state the options and your recommendation. Cite the file and line you are referring to so a reviewer can follow your reasoning. Once the task is done and tests pass, reply with a single concise summary line and stop. Avoid speculative refactors, defensive code for impossible states, and unrequested features. Verify your assumptions against the actual code rather than against what you expect to find.
user: Find out the current stable Rust edition, then check whether this repo's Cargo.toml already uses it, and report any mismatch.

function.name: read_file
function.description: Read the full UTF-8 contents of a file at the given repository-relative path and return it verbatim with line numbers.

function.name: write_file
function.description: Create or overwrite a file at the given path with the provided content, creating any missing parent directories.

function.name: grep
function.description: Search file contents across the repository using a regular expression and return matching lines with their file and line number.

glob.description: optional path glob to restrict the search

function.name: list_dir
function.description: List the entries of a directory, optionally recursively, returning names, types, and sizes.

function.name: run_tests
function.description: Run the project's test suite and return a pass or fail summary plus any failing test output.

filter.description: optional substring to select which tests run

function.name: run_bash
function.description: Execute a single shell command in the project root and capture combined stdout and stderr.

function.name: web_search
function.description: Search the public web and return a ranked list of result titles, URLs, and short snippets.

function.name: git_log
function.description: Show recent commit history for a path, with hashes, authors, dates, and subjects.

path.description: optional path to scope the history

function.name: git_diff
function.description: Show the current uncommitted diff, optionally scoped to a path.

path.description: optional path to scope the diff

function.name: apply_patch
function.description: Apply a unified diff patch to the working tree and report which hunks succeeded or failed.

tool_stubs.web_search: Rust editions are 2015, 2018, 2021, and 2024. The current stable edition is 2024, stabilized in Rust 1.85.
